name: Elbmesh Architecture Boundary Checks
summary: Manual and automated checks for preserving Elbmesh Resource, Action, Event, Reaction, External Operation, View, Query, journal, and trace boundaries.

checks: Confirm each Action targets exactly one Resource and appends only that Resource's Events.
Confirm each Event has one owner Resource and explicit apply/replay logic when supported by current code.
Confirm replay/apply code does not call external systems.
Confirm execution failures are journaled or returned as errors rather than silently entering Resource streams.
Confirm Reactions invoke Actions and Views are rebuildable from Events where those runtime surfaces are touched.
Report missing automated checks separately from failed checks.

items.text: Every changed Action targets exactly one Resource.
Every changed Event belongs to exactly one Resource stream.
Replay/apply code is deterministic and has no external calls.
Action failures, runtime failures, Reaction failures, and External Operation failures do not pollute Resource Event streams unless explicitly modelled as domain Events.
Reactions invoke Actions rather than mutating Resources directly.
Views derive from Events and can be rebuilt from source Events.
Execution trace changes keep traces as derived visibility over Actions, Resource Events, Receipts, and journals.

blocks.markdown: Current code has executable checks for Action execution, Resource replay, Action Journal behavior, Projection/View runtime, Reaction runtime/journal behavior, and manifest validation. Some target architecture checks from docs and skills, such as detecting undeclared external HTTP calls or generated-doc drift, are not yet implemented as universal automated checks; reviewers must report those as manual or missing automated checks.

blocks.description: Review focus and must-flag architecture drift.
Target check-architecture command and core rules.
Current executable proofs for runtime behavior.
